questions.title: The Hook: What Is 鱼香肉丝?
questions.prompt: 鱼香肉丝 literally means “Fish-Fragrant Shredded Pork.” Take a guess — which of these dishes do you think it is?

options.text: Whole fish gently steamed with ginger, scallion, and a light soy drizzle.
Twice-cooked pork belly stir-fried with green peppers — smoky, rich, and savory.
Stir-fried shredded pork in a glossy reddish-brown sweet-sour-savory sauce — no fish at all.
Shredded beef quickly wok-tossed with fresh green chili strips.

questions.explanation: 鱼香 (yú-xiāng) means ‘fish-fragrant’ — but there is NO fish in this dish. It is a sweet-sour-savory-spicy sauce made with garlic, ginger, and pickled chilies. By the end of this lesson you’ll know exactly how to decode names like this.

questions.title: Quiz 1: What Is Hunan-style Stir-fried Beef?
questions.prompt: Each option shows a different beef preparation. Which one matches Hunan-style Stir-fried Beef?

options.text: Black pepper beef tenderloin — pan-seared strips in a savory dark sauce with onion.
Red-braised beef chunks slow-cooked in dark soy and rock sugar until glossy.
Sliced beef quickly wok-tossed with smoky dried chilies, garlic and scallion — dry, fragrant, pungently spicy.
Sliced beef simmered in a numbing Sichuan peppercorn and chili-oil broth.

questions.explanation: Hunan (湘) = chili-garlic-smoky, dry not saucy — direct chili heat with no numbing. Stir-fried (爆炒) = fast wok-toss giving fragrant, slightly charred edges. Together: a dry, intensely spicy plate. Black pepper (A) is a Western-influenced sauce style. Red-braised (B) is slow and saucy. Sichuan broth (D) numbs rather than burns.

questions.title: Quiz 2: What Is Cantonese Steamed Fish?
questions.prompt: Each option shows a different fish preparation. Which one matches Cantonese Steamed Fish?

options.text: Whole fish in a glossy sweet-sour vinegar sauce, a Hangzhou classic.
Whole fresh fish gently steamed with ginger and scallion, finished with hot oil and a light soy drizzle — clean and delicate.
Fish fillet in a deep red bowl of bubbling chili-oil broth with peppercorns.
Paper-thin slices of fresh fish served with ginger, scallion and sesame oil.

questions.explanation: Cantonese (粤) = light, fresh, ingredient-forward. Steamed (蒸) = gentle moist heat, no oil crust, no heavy sauce. Together: a clean pale dish with simple aromatics. West Lake fish (A) is Hangzhou-style with sauce. Sichuan water-boiled (C) is intensely spicy. Shunde raw fish (D) is served uncooked.

questions.title: Final Scenario: Your Friend Cannot Eat Spicy Food
questions.prompt: Your friend cannot eat spicy food. You are ordering together at a Chinese restaurant. Select ALL dishes they must AVOID.

options.name: Hunan Steamed Cured Pork
options.text: Hunan Steamed Cured Pork (湘式蒸腊肉)
options.detail: Hunan (湘) prefix = chili-garlic-smoky. The cured pork is steamed with dried chilies and garlic — direct, unforgiving heat.

options.name: Mala Maoxuewang
options.detail: Mala (麻辣) = numbing + burning. A Sichuan hotpot-style dish with two layers of spice: chili burn and peppercorn numbness.

options.name: Cantonese White-cut Chicken
options.detail: Cantonese = light / fresh. Poached in plain water with ginger and scallion, served with a mild dipping sauce. No chili.

options.name: Fish-Fragrant Eggplant
options.detail: 鱼香 = sweet-sour-savory with mild pickled chili. Much gentler than Hunan or mala — the chili is in the sauce, not the heat.

questions.explanation: Hunan (A) and mala (B) are both high-heat styles your friend must avoid. Cantonese white-cut chicken (C) is mild. Fish-fragrant eggplant (D) uses 鱼香 sauce which has pickled chili but is far milder — and now you know what 鱼香 actually means!
